articles.title: Acme Textiles faces NCLT petition from operational creditor over â‚¹2.3 Cr dues
articles.raw_text: An operational creditor, Bhiwandi Yarn Traders, has filed a petition before NCLT Mumbai Bench against Acme Textiles Ltd citing outstanding dues of â‚¹2.3 Crore. The company has denied the claim stating it is disputed and under arbitration. The next hearing is scheduled for December 2024.

articles.title: Promoter of Acme Textiles pledges additional 12% shares to secure working capital loan
articles.raw_text: Ramesh Agarwal, Managing Director of Acme Textiles Ltd, has pledged an additional 12% of his shareholding to raise working capital from a private lender. Total promoter pledge now stands at 68% of promoter holding, raising concerns among minority shareholders.

articles.title: Indian textile sector facing headwinds from weak export demand and rising cotton prices
articles.raw_text: Indian textile manufacturers are facing a dual challenge: export volumes to Europe and the US have declined 8-12% due to weak consumer demand, while domestic cotton prices remain elevated. Industry body AEPC expects recovery only in H2 FY2025. Small and mid-sized players with high debt are at heightened risk.

articles.title: CARE Ratings revises Acme Textiles outlook to Negative on tightening liquidity
articles.raw_text: CARE Ratings has revised the credit outlook for Acme Textiles Ltd from Stable to Negative while maintaining the BBB- rating. The revision reflects the company's tightening liquidity position, high promoter pledge levels, and a pending NCLT matter. CARE will review the rating after the December 2024 NCLT hearing.

articles.title: Acme Textiles commissions new ring-frame spinning unit, increases capacity by 20%
articles.raw_text: Acme Textiles Ltd has successfully commissioned a new ring-frame spinning unit at its Bhiwandi facility, increasing installed spinning capacity by 20%. The â‚¹8 Cr capex was funded through a mix of internal accruals and term debt.
